name: Drone
description: Consumer and professional unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). Rules vary by airline and depend on battery size.
tsaGuideline: Drones are allowed through TSA security checkpoints. The lithium batteries powering them must comply with airline lithium battery rules.
iataGuideline: Drone batteries follow lithium battery regulations. Spare batteries must be in carry-on. Drone itself may go in checked or carry-on.
capacityLimit: Battery must be ≤100Wh (carry-on without approval); spare batteries must be in carry-on
restrictions: Drone batteries (spare) must be in carry-on
Drone body may be checked or carried on, subject to airline policy
Some airlines restrict drones entirely — check before flying
Batteries over 100Wh require prior airline approval

airlineRules.notes: Follow lithium battery rules for batteries.
Drone body allowed; batteries must follow lithium battery policy.
Contact Emirates before travel. Batteries ≤100Wh in carry-on.

faqs.question: Can I bring a drone on a plane?
faqs.answer: Generally yes, but it depends on the airline and the drone's battery. The drone body can usually be checked or carried on. The batteries — especially spare ones — must go in your carry-on bag and comply with lithium battery watt-hour limits.

faqs.question: Do I need to declare my drone at the airport?
faqs.answer: Not typically for TSA screening in the US, but some countries require declaration at customs. Always check destination country rules.
